name: Chocolate Cherry Torte 02 - Country Living
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut the chocolate cake into three 9 by 5-inch rectangles using the bottom of a 9 by 5-inch loaf pan as a pattern.
2. Use leftover cake trimmings for snacking.
3. Line the inside of the loaf pan with plastic wrap, allowing 2 inches to extend over the sides so assembled torte can be easily removed from the pan.
4. Place one cake rectangle in the bottom of the pan and brush generously with some Coffee Syrup.
5. Spread half of the cheese filling on top of the cake in the pan.
6. Place another cake rectangle atop the filling and brush with Coffee Syrup.
7. Top with cherry filling.
8. Place remaining cake atop cherry filling and brush with remaining Coffee Syrup, spreading smoothly as this will be the top of the torte.
9. Cover the torte with overhanging plastic wrap and refrigerate at least 4 hours or overnight.
10. Prepare Chocolate Meringue Twigs.
11. To serve, remove the torte from the pan by pulling up the plastic wrap on all sides.
12. Place the torte on a serving plate and gently pull the plastic wrap to remove it underneath.
13. Gently press flat sides of chocolate twigs upright onto the sides of the torte to cover completely.
14. If desired, tie ribbon around the sides of the torte, ending in a bow in the center of one long side.
15. Top with fresh cherries if desired and serve. Alternatively, refrigerate up to 2 hours before serving.
Chocolate Meringue Twigs:
1. Preheat oven to 275°F (145°C).
2. Line two large baking sheets with parchment paper or aluminum foil and grease lightly.
3. In a medium-size bowl, mix together 1/2 cup sugar and 2 large egg whites.
4. Set the bowl in a larger bowl of very hot, not boiling, water.
5. Beat the mixture with a portable electric mixer at high speed until thick and nearly double in volume.
6. Remove the bowl from the hot water and continue beating the egg whites until very stiff peaks form (about 4 minutes).
7. Sift 2 tablespoons unsweetened cocoa powder over the meringue and fold it in until blended.
8. Transfer the chocolate meringue to a pastry bag fitted with a 3/8-inch round tip.
9. Pipe 3 to 4-inch long "twigs" of meringue onto the baking sheets, making narrow rows.
10. Bake the meringue twigs for 1 1/2 hours or until dry and firm.
11. Cool the twigs completely on the baking sheets on wire racks.
12. Meanwhile, melt 1 1/2 ounces bittersweet chocolate in a microwave or over simmering water.
13. Dip a fork in the melted chocolate and quickly move it back and forth across the twigs to create thin chocolate stripes.
14. Refrigerate the twigs just until the chocolate stripes harden (about 5 minutes).
15. Gently remove the twigs from parchment paper and store them in an airtight container, unrefrigerated, until ready to use.
Note: Dried cherries are available by mail from American Spoon Foods, 1668 Clarion Ave., P.O. Box 566, Petosky, MI 49770-0566; (800) 222-5886.
